resources.string: flipx requires Shizuku to run. Shizuku is a free third-party service that grants flipx the shell-level access it needs to read the hinge\'s input device. It does not require root.
Shizuku is installed but not currently running. Open Shizuku and start it (the app explains the one-time ADB pairing flow).
Shizuku is running. Grant flipx permission to use it.
Ready. Tap to start the hinge watcher.
Watcher is active. Pick your launchers below, then tap \"Make flipx the home\".
On hinge OPEN, route home to:
On hinge CLOSE, route home to:
Tip: if ES-DE has a \"launcher mode\" QS tile, turn it OFF — flipx should be the only app holding the HOME role.

When on: closing the hinge locks landscape; opening the hinge locks 90° counter-clockwise of that. Both directions stay locked so routed apps don\'t auto-rotate. When off, flipx never touches rotation.
